pairs.question: Who was Joseph Thomas Ferraracci and what political position did he hold?
pairs.answer: Joseph Thomas Ferraracci was an American politician who served as a member of the Maryland Senate representing District 8 from 2002 to 2003. District 8 covers portions of Baltimore County and Baltimore.

pairs.question: How did Joseph Thomas Ferraracci become a Maryland State Senator?
pairs.answer: Ferraracci was appointed to the Maryland Senate in 2002 following the death of John R. Schneider, who had been appointed to replace Thomas L. Bromwell. Bromwell had resigned to accept a position with the Maryland Injured Workers Insurance Fund. Ferraracci was appointed by Governor Parris Glendening to serve out the remainder of the term.

pairs.question: What was Ferraracci's involvement in the Baltimore County Democratic Central Committee?
pairs.answer: Ferraracci was a member of the Baltimore County Democratic Central Committee since 1982. He served as chair of the committee from 1994 to 1996 and resumed the position in 2002.

pairs.question: What other political roles did Ferraracci run for besides the Maryland Senate?
pairs.answer: In 2006, Ferraracci ran for a position on the Orphans Court but received the fewest votes among the 11 candidates.

pairs.question: What was Ferraracci's military service background?
pairs.answer: Ferraracci served in the U.S. Army Reserves for eleven years during his career.

pairs.question: When did Joseph Thomas Ferraracci pass away and what was his age at the time of death?
pairs.answer: Ferraracci died on August 25, 2023. He was 86 years old at the time of his death.

pairs.question: Who succeeded Joseph Thomas Ferraracci in the Maryland Senate after his term ended?
pairs.answer: Kathy Klausmeier succeeded Ferraracci in the Maryland Senate. She won the Democratic primary and general election in 2002 and was sworn in by 2003.
